none.justification: Badly incomplete: it claims USE_JEMALLOC appears only in zmalloc.c/debug.c and lists only 4 sites, missing the many src/ files and the Makefile build-system effect called out by the GT. It does classify its small subset correctly, and notes HAVE_BACKTRACE is in debug.c/crash reporting, but also undercounts it versus the GT.

ccodegraph.justification: Same core failure as Answer A: it asserts only two files and 4 USE_JEMALLOC sites, missing most allocator-gated files plus the required Makefile jemalloc build/link behavior. The subsystem classification for the subset it found is correct, but the inventory is far too incomplete.

codegraph.justification: Mostly correct: it adds the important #if defined(USE_JEMALLOC) sites across the Redis src tree and mentions Makefile involvement, with correct allocator classification. Real gaps remain: it still frames the strict #ifdef result as only 4, undercounts HAVE_BACKTRACE as 10 instead of the GT's 15 occurrences, misses some zmalloc.c details, and only lightly covers the full Makefile build/link/dependency effect.

cbm.justification: Badly incomplete: it reports only the 4 exact USE_JEMALLOC sites in zmalloc.c/debug.c and misses the broader allocator-gated source files and Makefile behavior required by the GT. Its classification of those found sites and HAVE_BACKTRACE as debug.c crash reporting is correct, but the answer is not close to a complete inventory.
